remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Acute insomnia (<3 months): 50-60% resolve spontaneously; chronic insomnia: only 20-25% spontaneous remission at 1 year; persistent insomnia in 50-75% without treatment
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I): 70-80% response rate; 40-60% remission (normalised sleep); durable at 6-24 months post-treatment; superior to sleep medication in long-term outcomes; digital CBT-I (Sleepio, Somryst) equivalent efficacy to in-person
remission.gap_size: Very large — CBT-I is guideline-recommended as first-line but almost universally unavailable; medication is default
remission.barrier_detail: AASM and most sleep guidelines recommend CBT-I as first-line over medication for chronic insomnia; fewer than 1% of insomnia sufferers have access to a CBT-I trained therapist; digital CBT-I exists and works but is rarely prescribed; benzodiazepines and z-drugs continue to dominate prescribing despite guidelines against long-term use
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — FDA approved daridorexant (Quviviq — orexin receptor antagonist); 2019 lemborexant; 2014 suvorexant (first orexin antagonist); z-drugs (zopiclone, zolpidem) since 1990s; benzodiazepines for sleep since 1960s
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term benzodiazepine prescribing for insomnia (STOPP criteria: inappropriate in elderly; falls, cognitive impairment, dependence; yet ~15% of elderly adults in US are on chronic BZD); OTC antihistamines for chronic insomnia (diphenhydramine — rapid tolerance, next-day cognitive impairment); trazodone at sedating doses (efficacy data for insomnia very limited but extremely common off-label)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Sleep restriction therapy as standalone (abbreviated CBT-I delivered in primary care — non-inferior to full CBT-I in RESTING trial); stimulus control instructions; bright light therapy for circadian phase disorders; magnesium glycinate (modest but reproducible improvement in sleep quality in trials); L-theanine + GABA combination
remission.frontier_signal: Digital therapeutics (Somryst — FDA De Novo approved 2020, prescription digital CBT-I); orexin dual antagonists (daridorexant showing less rebound insomnia vs older agents); cannabinoids (CBD for REM sleep behaviour disorder — Phase 2); personalized chronobiology — light-based circadian recalibration protocols
